adds.rationale: Pure-play quantum-computing exposure at a commercial inflection: IonQ disclosed it had reached its three-year $100M cumulative-bookings target ahead of plan, the only watchlist gap against the profile's named next-waves (rockets covered by RKLB, nuclear by URA, robotics by BOTZ, AI by NVDA).

news_evidence.summary: IonQ Q2 2023 results: company on track to exceed $100M cumulative bookings target by year-end 2023, with revenue growth above 100% year-over-year.
Quantum computing sector transitioning from proof-of-concept to deployed systems and real-world applications across IonQ, Rigetti, D-Wave.

removes.rationale: At a 5-name watchlist cap, a broad robotics ETF is the weakest slot to hold: the robotics wave is still slow-buildup, BOTZ's top holdings overlap NVDA exposure already in the list, and freeing the slot lets a pure-play next-wave name (quantum) enter without violating the cap.

news_evidence.summary: BOTZ remains a diversified robotics/AI thematic ETF whose largest weights (NVDA, Intuitive Surgical, ABB, Keyence) duplicate AI exposure already held via NVDA directly, with no 2023 catalyst lifting it above pure-play alternatives.

rationale_overall: Single-swap rebalance against a hard 5-name cap: trade a broad robotics ETF (BOTZ) for a pure-play quantum name (IONQ) that fills the only thesis-named next-wave bucket currently unrepresented. Post-change watchlist preserves one ticker per major bucket — AI (NVDA), nuclear (URA), rockets (RKLB), geopolitical-energy (LNG), quantum (IONQ) — keeping the portfolio aligned with the profile's stated dominant wave (AI) plus four early-stage next-waves with no thematic stacking.
